water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River offers fair to good trout fishing with warming water temperatures and recent stocking events enhancing the bite. Trout are likely to be found in oxygenated runs and pocket water using natural and attractor patterns.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning is the best window as water temperatures are warming which encourages trout to favor oxygenated runs, combined with recent stocking improving trout activity and receptiveness to stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns and squirmy worms.
generated_text.current_conditions: The river near Norcross, GA has a water temperature of 62.6°F, flow at 899 cfs, gage height of 2.03 ft, and turbidity of 5.8 NTU as of the latest USGS readings.
generated_text.weather_setup: Conditions show light sky cover averaging 19.62%, steady pressure at 30.04 inHg with no clear trend, a west wind averaging 5.6 mph, and a low rain probability of 1-27% with minimal expected precipitation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position in riffles, pocket water, runs, seams, and other oxygenated or structured areas. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns early or late in the day, and incorporate stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns and durable nymphs. Caution is advised as pressure trends are unavailable; remain adaptable to changing conditions.
